title: Video Recommendation System - System Design Interview
tagline: Design a personalized homepage recommendation system using hybrid candidate generation and ranking.
problem: Build a homepage video recommendation system for a YouTube-like product with global users, multilingual videos, 10 billion videos, interaction-based training data, and a 200 ms serving budget.

functional: Handle global users and multilingual videos.
Support new users and new videos.
Measure recommendation quality online and offline.
requirements.designPrompt: Keep the recommendation stack clear: data logging, candidate generation, ranking, serving cache, exploration/cold start, and evaluation.

capacity.label: Candidate count
capacity.value: Generate hundreds to thousands, rank tens to hundreds.

capacity.label: Serving latency
capacity.value: Most expensive retrieval and feature work must be precomputed or cached.

capacity.label: Objective
capacity.value: Blend watch time, satisfaction, explicit feedback, and guardrails.

capacity.label: Cold start
capacity.value: Use content features, trends, locale, and exploration.

capacity.label: Core assumptions
capacity.note: 10 billion videos in the corpus. Recommendations are personalized for logged-in homepage users. Interaction logs include impressions, clicks, watch time, completion, likes, and hides. Serving target is under 200 ms.

capacity.label: Design focus
capacity.note: At 10B videos, scoring everything online is impossible. The design must use retrieval plus ranking, with precomputed features and candidate caches.

dataModel.name: video
dataModel.note: Recommendable video metadata and content features.

steps.title: 1. Define Recommendation Objective
steps.description: The goal is sustained user engagement and satisfaction, not just clicks. The objective should combine watch behavior, explicit feedback, and guardrails against clickbait.

concepts.definition: The target used to order videos for users.
concepts.whyItMatters: It shapes all model behavior.

concepts.definition: How long users watch after recommendation.
concepts.whyItMatters: It is useful but can overfavor long videos.

concepts.definition: A metric that prevents optimizing one signal at user expense.
concepts.whyItMatters: It helps avoid clickbait and low-quality engagement.

deepDives: Pure completion can overfavor short videos; pure watch time can overfavor long videos.
bottlenecks: Bad objective design causes bad recommendations at scale.

steps.title: 2. Build Interaction Logs and Features
steps.description: The system logs impressions and outcomes, then builds user, video, creator, language, and context features for training and serving.
steps.decisionPrompt: What data is needed to train and serve personalized recommendations?

concepts.definition: A record that a video was shown.
concepts.whyItMatters: It prevents training only on clicked positives.

concepts.definition: A vector summarizing user preferences.
concepts.whyItMatters: It supports collaborative and content matching.

concepts.definition: A vector summarizing video content and audience behavior.
concepts.whyItMatters: It lets recommendation models compare videos and users.

deepDives: Features must be available offline for training and online for serving.
talkingPoints: Separate user features from video features.

steps.title: 3. Generate Candidates With Collaborative Signals
steps.description: Candidate generation retrieves videos watched by similar users, videos co-watched with recent history, subscriptions, trends, and content-similar items.
steps.decisionPrompt: How do we find a manageable set of plausible videos from 10 billion?

concepts.definition: Using behavior of similar users or items.
concepts.whyItMatters: It captures taste patterns not obvious from metadata.

concepts.definition: A fast recall-oriented retrieval stage.
concepts.whyItMatters: It makes ranking feasible.

concepts.definition: One retrieval strategy contributing videos.
concepts.whyItMatters: Multiple sources improve coverage.

deepDives: Candidate generation maximizes recall; ranking will sort and filter.

steps.title: 4. Rank Candidates With Content and User Features
steps.description: The ranker predicts satisfaction for each user-video pair using collaborative, content, creator, freshness, language, and context features.
steps.decisionPrompt: What should the ranking model know about the user and each candidate video?

concepts.definition: Combining collaborative and content-based signals.
concepts.whyItMatters: It works better for both known tastes and new content.

concepts.definition: Training a model to order candidates.
concepts.whyItMatters: It optimizes the final homepage order.

concepts.definition: A feature expressing interaction between user and item attributes.
concepts.whyItMatters: It captures match quality such as user language versus video language.

deepDives: The ranking model should predict multiple outcomes or a calibrated blended score rather than only click probability.
bottlenecks: Ranking too many candidates breaches latency.

steps.title: 5. Assemble a Hybrid Serving Pipeline
steps.description: The online request gets cached candidates, fetches fresh features, scores in batch, applies rules, hydrates video cards, and returns the homepage.
steps.decisionPrompt: Which work belongs online and which work should be precomputed?

concepts.definition: Precomputed candidates stored for users or cohorts.
concepts.whyItMatters: It reduces request-time retrieval cost.

concepts.definition: Scoring many candidates in one model call.
concepts.whyItMatters: It lowers model serving overhead.

concepts.definition: Business and product constraints after scoring.
concepts.whyItMatters: They enforce diversity, freshness, and safety guardrails.

flows.name: Homepage request
flows.note: Serving uses precomputed candidates and online ranking to stay inside the latency budget.

deepDives: Precomputing candidates does not mean final ranking is stale; the ranker can use fresh features online.
bottlenecks: Candidate cache misses for new or inactive users.

steps.title: 6. Meet the 200 ms Latency Budget
steps.description: Use offline batch features for stable signals, nearline updates for recent behavior, online caches for hot users, and bounded candidate counts for ranking.
steps.decisionPrompt: How does the system avoid expensive computation during the request?

concepts.definition: A feature computed periodically over large data.
concepts.whyItMatters: It is cheap to serve but less fresh.

concepts.definition: A feature updated from streams soon after events occur.
concepts.whyItMatters: It captures recent interests.

concepts.definition: A maximum number of candidates scored online.
concepts.whyItMatters: It protects latency.

deepDives: A 200 ms target forces most graph mining and embedding work out of the request path.
talkingPoints: Mention fallback recommendations when features are missing.

steps.title: 7. Handle Cold Start and Exploration
steps.description: New users can receive locale, trend, and onboarding-based recommendations. New videos can be explored through content features and controlled traffic allocation.
steps.decisionPrompt: How do recommendations work when little interaction data exists?

concepts.definition: The lack of historical data for a user or item.
concepts.whyItMatters: It is a central recommender-system challenge.

concepts.definition: Showing some uncertain items to learn their quality.
concepts.whyItMatters: It gathers data beyond current model preferences.

concepts.definition: Avoiding too many similar recommendations.
concepts.whyItMatters: It improves discovery and reduces repetition.

deepDives: Exploration should be budgeted, not random chaos across the whole homepage.
bottlenecks: Too much exploration harms short-term engagement.
Too little exploration starves new creators.
talkingPoints: Use content-based ranking for new videos before collaborative signals exist.

steps.title: 8. Evaluate and Operate Recommendation Models
steps.description: Offline metrics, replay evaluation, and online experiments govern rollout. Monitoring tracks latency, engagement, satisfaction, diversity, and model/data drift.
steps.decisionPrompt: How do we know a recommendation model is safe to launch?

concepts.definition: Testing a model on held-out historical data.
concepts.whyItMatters: It filters bad candidates before live traffic.

concepts.definition: A controlled rollout to real users.
concepts.whyItMatters: It measures actual product impact.

concepts.definition: A decline caused by changing data or user behavior.
concepts.whyItMatters: It tells when retraining or rollback is needed.

deepDives: Guardrails should include hides, quick abandons, diversity, latency, and creator ecosystem health, not only watch time.
bottlenecks: Feedback loops can overfit to what the current recommender already shows.
talkingPoints: Close by explaining the training-serving feedback loop.

functional.how: Candidate generators produce plausible videos, the ranker scores user-video pairs, and post-processing returns a diverse homepage.
Impressions, clicks, watch time, likes, hides, and completions feed feature pipelines and model evaluation.
Candidate generation and caches avoid scoring the whole corpus, while bounded batch inference keeps request latency under control.
New users and new videos fall back to content features, regional trends, locale, subscriptions, and controlled exploration.

nonFunctional.how: Precomputed candidates, online feature stores, batch model serving, and metadata hydration budgets protect the 200 ms target.
Offline evaluation, A/B experiments, guardrail metrics, and diversity rules prevent pure clickbait optimization.
Nearline features and exploration budgets help the system learn recent interests and give new videos measured exposure.

finalDesign.title: Final Design
finalDesign.description: The final design is a hybrid recommender with offline learning and online ranking. Interaction logs build user and video features, candidate generators retrieve plausible videos from collaborative and content sources, candidate caches reduce request cost, the online ranker scores bounded candidates within 200 ms, rules handle diversity and exploration, and experiments control model rollout.
finalDesign.decisionPrompt: Walk through a homepage recommendation request, then explain how impressions and watch outcomes improve the next model version.

concepts.definition: Collaborative signals and content signals work together.
concepts.whyItMatters: It handles mature users, new videos, multilingual content, and cold start better than either alone.

concepts.definition: Precomputed candidate sets for users or cohorts.
concepts.whyItMatters: It moves expensive retrieval out of the 200 ms request path.

concepts.definition: A controlled slice of recommendations used to learn about uncertain videos.
concepts.whyItMatters: It prevents the system from starving new creators or overfitting old preferences.

options.title: Full Final Design
options.description: Complete architecture combining every major step from the interview. This is the default wrap-up view.

options.title: Cold-start heavy variant
options.description: Useful for anonymous or new users, but weaker than the hybrid design once collaborative interaction history exists.

flows.name: Homepage serving
flows.note: The online request reads cached candidates, joins fresh features, scores in batch, applies rules, and returns a page.

flows.name: Learning loop
flows.note: Impressions and watch outcomes update features and train candidate and ranking models.

deepDives: The recommender answer is incomplete without both candidate generation and ranking. The 200 ms budget is what forces the split.
bottlenecks: Feedback loops can overexpose already popular videos.
talkingPoints: Avoid pure click, pure completion, or pure watch-time objectives.
Use cold-start fallbacks for new users and videos.
Measure satisfaction and guardrails in experiments.

followUps: How would you tune exploration safely?
How do you avoid recommending harmful clickbait?
How do you support multilingual recommendations?
What happens when the feature store is unavailable?

api.description: Precompute candidates for a user or cohort.
Roll out ranking or candidate model versions.

description: Design a personalized homepage recommendation system using hybrid candidate generation and ranking.
